framing: Management consultant transitioning to programme manager role at international development NGO in Nairobi

jd.title: Programme Manager — Digital Health
jd.style_note: Graduate-friendly/NGO: mission-driven, explains impact, values-based requirements
jd.about: PATH is a global nonprofit dedicated to health equity. We're looking for a Programme Manager to lead our East Africa digital health portfolio, working with ministries of health to strengthen health information systems and expand mobile health (mHealth) solutions.

requirements.text: 5+ years of experience in programme management, health systems strengthening, or digital health implementation in low- and middle-income countries (LMICs)
Experience working with government stakeholders (Ministry of Health, county health departments) in East Africa
Knowledge of health information systems (DHIS2, OpenMRS, or CommCare)
Experience managing donor-funded programmes (USAID, BMGF, Global Fund, or DFID/FCDO)
Strong M&E (monitoring and evaluation) skills using logical frameworks and theory of change
Experience with budget management for programmes of $2M+ annually
Master's degree in Public Health, International Development, or related field
Familiarity with WHO digital health guidelines and the Principles for Digital Development
Proficiency in Swahili is an advantage

responsibilities: Lead implementation of USAID-funded digital health programme across 12 counties in Kenya
Manage relationships with Kenya Ministry of Health, county health management teams, and implementing partners
Oversee deployment and scale-up of DHIS2-based health information systems
Coordinate M&E activities and produce quarterly donor reports
Manage programme budget (USD 3.2M annually) and sub-grantee financial oversight
Represent PATH at health sector coordination meetings and donor forums

expected_output.summary: Management consultant with 7 years at McKinsey's Social Sector Practice transitioning to international development programme management. Led health system strategy engagements for 3 African governments (Kenya, Nigeria, Ethiopia) and the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation. MPH from Johns Hopkins with thesis on mHealth adoption in East Africa. Seeks to move from advisory to implementation, applying strategy and stakeholder management skills to direct programme delivery.

experience.title: Engagement Manager
bullets: Led health system strategy engagements for Kenya Ministry of Health (Universal Health Coverage roadmap) and Nigeria Federal Ministry of Health (PHC revitalisation)
Managed 4-person McKinsey teams across 6-month engagements with government and donor stakeholders
Developed digital health investment case for BMGF resulting in $15M funding allocation for DHIS2 scale-up across 3 countries
Designed M&E frameworks for 2 national health programmes using theory of change methodology
Built financial models and budget scenarios for donor-funded programmes (USAID, BMGF)

experience.title: Associate
bullets: Led workstreams on healthcare and public sector engagements across East Africa and Middle East
Managed day-to-day client relationships with government health officials in Kenya and Ethiopia
Developed analytical frameworks for Ethiopian health workforce planning study (100+ stakeholder interviews)

experience.title: Business Analyst
bullets: Supported healthcare and public sector engagements in East Africa and Middle East as junior team member
Conducted primary research and stakeholder interviews for health system strategy projects
Built data models in Excel and Tableau for health system performance dashboards

education: MPH — Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health (2016-2018)

jd_requirements: 5+ years programme management health systems digital health LMICs
government stakeholders Ministry of Health East Africa
DHIS2 OpenMRS CommCare health information systems
donor-funded programmes USAID BMGF Global Fund DFID FCDO
M&E monitoring evaluation logical frameworks theory of change
WHO digital health guidelines Principles for Digital Development
deliberate_flaws: No direct programme implementation experience — all advisory/consulting (McKinsey advises, doesn't implement)
No hands-on DHIS2 experience (advised on investment, never deployed or configured it)
No sub-grantee financial management experience (modelled budgets, didn't manage them)
No USAID compliance experience (FAR, ADS 303, cost principles) — only built budget scenarios
Career changer — consulting to NGO culture shock (pace, hierarchy, compensation)
No Swahili proficiency despite being Kenyan (university-educated in English)
WHO digital health guidelines and Principles for Digital Development — awareness only, not applied

provenance.notes: Synthetic persona and JD. JD style modeled from real Greenhouse/Lever postings. Expected output and cloud_skills are AI-generated. 4/8 scorecard checks (jd_coverage, no_fabrication, metrics_preserved, no_fabricated_skills) evaluate AI vs AI reference. 4/8 checks (factual_preservation, word_count, ats_structure, action_verbs) are structurally valid regardless.
